title: Call-graph producer refactor and generative AST contract tests
goal: Reduce stateful visitor and validation duplication while preserving the exact v1 artifact semantics, then falsify range, scope and binding assumptions with generated AST edge cases.

acceptance.assertion: Scope collection, call recording and resolution are separated behind typed immutable records without increasing the maintainability ratchet or changing public artifact fields.
Producer, schema and consumer share one contract-near normalization and validation path or generated conformance vectors that prevent divergent range and diagnostic rules.

Property-based or grammar-generated tests cover missing and malformed AST end positions, nested scopes, comprehensions, decorators, defaults, shadowing, duplicate definitions and package/module collisions with bounded reproducible seeds.
Golden v1 fixtures and prior valid bundles remain readable; any contract revision is explicit, migration-tested and does not retroactively invalidate v1 evidence.

metadata.source_request: Post-merge reviews for Lenskit PR #1018 were live-verified; confirmed optimization work is registered separately from the corrective follow-up.
metadata.review_origin: Lenskit PR #1018 post-merge maintainability and testability review

bureau_always_open_scope.reason: Keep Bureau intake and unrelated registry writes available while Lenskit implementation runs in an isolated worktree.
